description: Per-field validators — failing surface (§55). Typing "A" fails length(>=2); signup.name.isValid + signup.isValid go false and the <errors> anchor renders an error. OQ1 NOTE: the whole-tree dom is DELIBERATELY OMITTED here because the rendered error MESSAGE text is explicit D3 impl-freedom; the impl-neutral contract is the validity-surface STATE (isValid false) + the error TAG.
rationale: §55 a failing validator drives isValid false and populates the errors list (semantic tag LengthFailed). The displayed message string is impl-freedom (asserted via state, never DOM text). This case is the OQ1 exhibit for 'whole-tree mode is unsuitable when an impl-freedom message is rendered'.
